title: grewia rothii
intro: Grewia rothii, also known as the Indian plum or forest berry, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Grewia rothii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or decoctions, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and natural remedies.
